iteration1.notes: Baseline skill. csv-to-md lost points on total value calculation (wrong mental math) and column header casing test edge case.

iteration2.notes: Added CSV guidance with awk examples. Model didn't use them; same result as baseline.

iteration3.notes: Model copied broken awk syntax from skill and looped 28 times on the same error. Removed awk examples.

iteration4.notes: Simplified skill with no code examples, explicit mental math guidance. Model still computed wrong total value ($21,769.02 vs $46,993.75).

iteration5.notes: Instructed model to write Python script. Model wrote broken f-strings with unclosed braces, timed out. Reverted to iteration 4 version.

changes: Simplified skill from verbose to concise format
Added explicit guidance for CSV data report generation (mental math, capitalized headers)
Added instructions for counting unique values carefully
Removed code examples (awk/python) that caused model to loop on syntax errors
Kept markitdown usage guidance for HTML conversion
limitations: qwen3-30b cannot reliably compute price*stock multiplication and summation for 10 rows
qwen3-30b cannot write valid Python f-strings through JSON serialization (nested curly braces)
qwen3-30b copies code examples verbatim without fixing syntax errors, leading to infinite retry loops
The main-table test uses a JavaScript || pattern that evaluates both sides, failing when 'Price' (capitalized) is not in the document even though 'price' (lowercase) is present
